title: PhD
biography: Moncef Slaoui is a Moroccan-Belgian-American immunologist who served as chief scientific advisor for Operation Warp Speed, the US government program that developed COVID-19 vaccines in record time. Previously he led vaccine development at GlaxoSmithKline for 30 years, overseeing 14 new vaccines including those for cervical cancer and malaria.
aiSummary: Moncef Slaoui is an immunology specialist who led Operation Warp Speed for COVID-19 vaccine development. Oversaw 14 new vaccines at GSK over 30 years. Based in Philadelphia, United States.
